threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 10-minute walk is too far and ask locals for directions to the nearest tube station.
Last night on the 333, a bloke argued passionately with the driver about the best Jamaican jerk. Didn't stop until we hit Streatham. Classic Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using a cardboard box as a makeshift sled down the hill in Hasenheide park. Pure joy in the chaos of the city.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that opens at 5 AM for the after-party crowd, serving sauce-saturated dürüm while locals stumble in, still buzzing.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they don’t flinch at a BART delay but lose their minds over a dog on the tracks at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 at Civic Center just as a guy with a full saxophone ensemble set up to busk. Muni delays turned into a spontaneous mini-concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the plazas and bars, but the real heartbeat is in the hidden alleys, where the old residents still hang laundry out to dry.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their hesitation at the tram stops; locals just glide on without checking the signs. It's a giveaway.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think saying 'hello' to strangers on the street is normal. Here, it’s just a nod and keep moving.
Caught a late 333 last week; the driver had a full-blown karaoke session with a group of tired clubbers. Best show of the night!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I spotted that old lady at the corner bakery, always haggling over a penny and leaving with a smile. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn stops seem to have a soundtrack? There's always someone playing a sax or a violin, like it’s a concert down there.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local when they dodge the dog on the tracks like it’s second nature, while newcomers just stare in confusion.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 49 during rush hour—three people singing 'We Are Family' while the bus crawled past a street musician. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a paella in Gràcia like on the beach. Most locals wouldn’t touch the stuff here. We're all about the tapas!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their puzzlement at the lack of a lift in many buildings - few locals bat an eye at the stairs to their flats.
